thresholds.name: knee_valgus
thresholds.device_validation: Above MediaPipe hip/knee noise floor (2.35-2.82° MAE controlled); real-world 5-10° error requires setup control (fitted clothing, good lighting, proper distance)

thresholds.name: knee_asymmetry
thresholds.clinical_significance: Contralateral compensation indicator; combined with hip drop suggests SBL involvement
thresholds.source: BrainLift v1 product spec; threshold derived from Hewett framework

thresholds.name: hip_drop
thresholds.clinical_significance: Indicates gluteus medius weakness or contralateral hip restriction; core driver in SBL compensation chains
thresholds.source: Clinical consensus; Ferber PFP study (PubMed 25102167) validates hip strength as upstream driver

thresholds.name: ankle_restriction
thresholds.clinical_significance: Ankle mobility blocks force transmission up kinetic chain; appears with knee valgus and hip drop on SBL
thresholds.source: BrainLift v1; MediaPipe ankle tracking unreliable (r=0.45 with occlusion); use with caution, surface in findings as lower-confidence

thresholds.name: trunk_lean
thresholds.value: >5° lateral during single-leg balance or squat
thresholds.clinical_significance: Indicates core instability or contralateral hip weakness; axial compensation pattern
thresholds.source: SFMA and dynamic movement screening literature; clinical standard

thresholds.name: hypermobility_adjustment
thresholds.value: When knee valgus <5° OR external rotation >45°, reduce/reverse threshold interpretation
thresholds.clinical_significance: Hypermobile athletes move differently (3.5° lower valgus, 4.5° greater ER baseline); fixed thresholds misclassify them
thresholds.source: PMC8558993, PMC9397026; critical for underrepresented population accuracy

chain_mappings.evidence_level: Strong (3/3 transitions verified across 14 studies — Wilke 2016)
chain_mappings.anatomical_sequence: plantar fascia → gastrocnemius → hamstrings → sacrotuberous ligament → erector spinae → thoracolumbar fascia → scalp fascia (galea aponeurotica)
chain_mappings.tcm_correspondence: Bladder Meridian (91% overlap per Dorsher 2009; 80-92% acupoint/fascial plane overlap per Langevin 2002)
chain_mappings.typical_compensation_pattern: ankle restriction + knee valgus + hip drop + contralateral trunk lean → SBL underactivity or overactivation

upstream_drivers_cc.effect: forces knee into valgus as compensation
increases lumbar demand, reduces hip extension available

citation_sources: Wilke et al. (2016) PubMed 26281953 — foundational systematic review
Kalichman (2025) — independent confirmation without citing Wilke
Dorsher (2009) Medical Acupuncture — 91% Bladder Meridian/SBL correspondence
Langevin & Yandow (2002) PubMed 12447927 — 80% acupoint/fascial plane overlap

chain_mappings.evidence_level: Strong (3/3 transitions verified across 8 studies — Wilke 2016)
chain_mappings.anatomical_sequence: latissimus dorsi → thoracolumbar fascia → contralateral gluteus maximus → vastus lateralis (via IT band/tensor fasciae latae)
chain_mappings.tcm_correspondence: Small Intestine and Triple Energizer meridians (composite; moderate evidence per Dorsher 2009)
chain_mappings.typical_compensation_pattern: shoulder depression + thoracic rotation limitation + contralateral hip weakness + knee valgus → BFL underactivity

upstream_drivers_cc.effect: shifts lat engagement; reduces thoracic stability
forces lumbar spine to compensate, destabilizes contralateral hip

upstream_drivers_cc.restriction_type: abduction weakness or external rotation limitation
upstream_drivers_cc.effect: increases demand on ipsilateral knee stabilizers, valgus collapse

citation_sources: Wilke et al. (2016) PubMed 26281953 — verified all 3 transitions
Myers Anatomy Trains framework — creator concedes evidence gaps (2018)

chain_mappings.evidence_level: Strong (2/2 transitions verified across 6 studies — Wilke 2016)
chain_mappings.anatomical_sequence: tibialis anterior → anterior tibialis fascia → quadriceps → rectus femoris → ilipsoas → pectoralis major/minor
chain_mappings.tcm_correspondence: Stomach and Heart meridians (composite; moderate evidence per Dorsher 2009)
chain_mappings.typical_compensation_pattern: ankle plantarflexion preference + knee extension or valgus + hip flexion dominance + shoulder protraction → FFL overactivity or imbalance with posterior chains

upstream_drivers_cc.effect: ankle compensates through plantarflexion; shifts knee into hyperextension or valgus
lumbar anterior translation; reduced hip extension available during loading
thoracic kyphosis increases; shifts load to lumbar chain

citation_sources: Wilke et al. (2016) PubMed 26281953 — verified 2/2 transitions

chain_mappings.rationale: Wilke 2016 and Kalichman 2025 independently converge on SBL/BFL/FFL as the only three chains with strong anatomical evidence. Restricting to these three is the only defensible position for clinical tool design.

cc_cp_logic.framework_name: Center of Coordination (CC) / Center of Perception (CP)
cc_cp_logic.source: Stecco Fascial Manipulation; Gnat 2022 RCT (MDPI Life 2022)

definition.center_of_perception_cp: Where pain/symptoms are felt. The complaint site. Where the user thinks the problem is.
definition.center_of_coordination_cc: Where the restriction/dysfunction lives. The upstream driver. What the screening tool identifies.

example_application.scenario: User reports knee pain during squat
example_application.app_traces_upstream: Knee valgus + ankle restriction + hip drop clustering on SBL
example_application.recommendation: Address ankle mobility first; measure progress; if no change, then hip mobility. Never treat only knee in isolation.
example_application.user_facing_language: Your knee compensates because your ankle and hip have limited range. We recommend addressing ankle mobility first because restricted dorsiflexion forces your knee into collapse during weight-bearing.

evidence_foundation.cc_cp_outcome: Stecco FM chain-reasoning treatment resolved pain in 1 session vs 3 sessions for local/symptom-focused treatment (Gnat 2022 RCT)
evidence_foundation.recurrence_reduction: 6.25% recurrence with upstream reasoning vs 50% with local-only (SFMA data); 8% vs 72% for lateral elbow
evidence_foundation.source: MDPI Life 2022 (https://www.mdpi.com/2075-1729/12/2/222); Austin Publishing 2024; RESTORE trial PubMed 37060913

cc_cp_logic.confidence_caveat: CC/CP framework is the encodable reasoning model, but chain-specificity remains a hypothesis the tool is positioned to test. Whole-body upstream reasoning shows superior outcomes vs local-only; proving WHICH SPECIFIC chain is involved is harder than v0 assumed (force transmission explains only ~10% of variance per Nature Sci Rep 2023).

citations.finding: Knee valgus >10° correlates with 2.5x ACL injury risk
citations.app_usage: Primary threshold for knee valgus detection; establishes clinical meaningfulness above noise floor

citations.finding: Hip strengthening resolves knee pain faster than knee-only treatment (n=199, pain resolved 1 week faster)
citations.app_usage: Evidence for upstream driver logic; validates hip as CC for knee CP

citations.finding: SFMA-guided treatment: 6.25% recurrence vs 50% for standard care
citations.app_usage: Demonstrates power of upstream reasoning; frames tool's value proposition

citations.finding: RESTORE trial (n=492): sustained 3-year improvement with upstream treatment, AU$5-8K cost savings
citations.app_usage: Long-term evidence for upstream reasoning; cost-effectiveness validation

citations.finding: Stecco Fascial Manipulation: chain-reasoning treatment 1 session vs 3 for local-only (Gnat 2022 RCT); greater magnitude and durability at 30-day
citations.app_usage: Direct evidence for CC/CP framework effectiveness; validates chain reasoning for faster resolution

citations.finding: SBL: 3/3 transitions verified across 14 cadaveric studies; BFL: 3/3 across 8 studies; FFL: 2/2 across 6 studies
citations.app_usage: Foundation for chain selection; only three chains with strong evidence

citations.finding: SFL (Superficial Front Line): 0 verified transitions across 7 cadaveric studies; no anatomical evidence
citations.app_usage: Justification for excluding SFL from tool logic

citations.finding: Kalichman (2025) independent narrative review arrives at identical hierarchy: SBL/BFL/FFL strong; Spiral/Lateral moderate; SFL none — WITHOUT citing Wilke
citations.app_usage: Validates chain selection; demonstrates convergence of evidence

citations.finding: Bladder Meridian (TCM) maps to SBL with 91% anatomical correspondence
citations.app_usage: Bridges TCM and Western fascial anatomy; adds depth to chain reasoning

citations.finding: 80% of acupuncture points coincide with intermuscular or intramuscular fascial planes
citations.app_usage: Validates fascial network density and clinical significance; supports chain reasoning mechanism

citations.finding: Hypermobile athletes show 3.5° lower minimum knee valgus and 4.5° greater peak external rotation during same movements
citations.app_usage: Critical for threshold adjustment; fixed thresholds misclassify hypermobile individuals

citations.finding: Poor posture does NOT predict pain; posture-pain causal link debunked
citations.app_usage: Validates decision to assess DYNAMIC movement, not static posture

citations.finding: Screening scores do NOT reliably predict injury (Bahr 2016 argument: no tool has validated all three steps)
citations.app_usage: Justifies educational framing; tool identifies PATTERNS, not injury predictions

citations.finding: AI gradient boosting classifiers detect compensatory movements more sensitively than experienced physiotherapists
citations.app_usage: Validates ML approach for pattern recognition; supports tool's clinical value

citations.finding: Zero cross-citations between Computer Vision and fascial chain research across 4,071 papers (Semantic Scholar, April 2026)
citations.source: BrainLift v1 — Semantic Scholar API analysis
citations.app_usage: Identifies integration gap; tool is first to bridge fields

citations.finding: Force transmission through fascia explains only ~10% of remote tissue displacement variance; within-chain ROM correlates weakly (r=0.32-0.44)
citations.source: Wilke et al. (2023) Nature Sci Rep; counter-evidence analysis
citations.app_usage: Frames chain-specificity as hypothesis, not proven mechanism; supports research-first positioning

citations.finding: FDA CDS Guidance (Jan 2026): clinical decision support can be educational without diagnostic claims
citations.app_usage: Legal framework for educational output framing; supports wellness positioning

citations.finding: Zero consumer apps with fascial chain reasoning (Frontiers 2025 systematic review of 8 apps)
citations.app_usage: Validates novelty; positions tool as first to integrate chain reasoning

mediapipe_performance.ankle_angle_mae: 3.06° (controlled; unreliable with occlusion, r=0.45 in forward fold)
mediapipe_performance.design_implication: Hip/knee adequate for >10° threshold detection. Ankle unreliable — flag all ankle findings as lower-confidence. Real-world setup guidance (fitted clothing, good lighting, proper distance) is essential, not optional.

confidence_coloring_system.green: High confidence: joint tracked with visibility >0.9 AND landmark displacement < 2° frame-to-frame
confidence_coloring_system.yellow: Medium confidence: joint visibility 0.7-0.9 OR displacement 2-5° frame-to-frame OR finding exceeds threshold by ≤50%
confidence_coloring_system.red: Low confidence: joint visibility <0.7 OR displacement >5° OR ankle-dependent finding OR finding just exceeds threshold AND MediaPipe collision/occlusion detected
confidence_coloring_system.application: Per-joint confidence colors displayed on skeleton during movement. Report findings only if green or yellow; red findings surfaced as 'tracking was unclear for this finding' with explicit lower-confidence language.

keyframe_snapshotting.method: Capture joint angles at peak movement (e.g., deepest squat depth) + 5-frame smoothing buffer to reduce noise
keyframe_snapshotting.rationale: Less noise than real-time frame analysis; clinically relevant (peak load is when compensation emerges)
keyframe_snapshotting.note: ML developer's scope for potential upgrade to continuous frame analysis or 3D angles

chain_attribution_accuracy.current_status: Unvalidated; subject to clinician validation during alpha testing
chain_attribution_accuracy.hypothesis: Co-occurring flags (knee valgus + hip drop + ankle restriction) cluster along documented fascial chains (SBL/BFL/FFL) more than random
chain_attribution_accuracy.alpha_validation: Clinicians will review 10 test subjects × 3 screenings each. Agreement metric: >=70% clinician agreement that chain attribution matches clinical assessment for the session.

existing_patterns.pattern: Overhead squat reveals ankle-knee-hip chain; forward fold reveals hamstring/SBL mobility; single-leg balance reveals hip abductor strength and core stability; overhead reach reveals shoulder/thoracic mobility and lat engagement
existing_patterns.source: FMS/SFMA clinical reasoning; movement screening literature
existing_patterns.implementation: Movement order: overhead squat → single-leg balance → overhead reach → forward fold. Preliminary findings between movements build narrative.

existing_patterns.pattern: Per-joint confidence colors (green/yellow/red) reduce extraneous cognitive load; users can instantly see tracking quality without reading numbers
existing_patterns.source: Cognitive Load Theory (Sweller); evidence from MediaPipe tracking studies showing importance of setup guidance
existing_patterns.implementation: Real-time skeleton overlay with joint-level confidence color coding; specific feedback for red joints ('move to better lighting' vs 'try removing jacket')

existing_patterns.pattern: Body-path language (knee → hip → lower back) translates clinical chain concepts to user understanding without requiring users to learn fascial anatomy
existing_patterns.source: Cognitive Load Theory; design for healthcare literacy variation
existing_patterns.implementation: Never use chain names (SBL, BFL, FFL) in user-facing output. Always convert to body-part connections with directional language.

existing_patterns.pattern: Layered report disclosure: summary first, details on expand; confidence colors on every finding; inline citations for evidence; conversation starters for clinician discussion
existing_patterns.source: BrainLift v1 cognitive design; health literacy research
existing_patterns.implementation: Summary page: 2-3 sentence overview of findings. Each finding expandable: detail, confidence, evidence citation, next-step suggestion.

constraints.constraint: MediaPipe ankle tracking is unreliable (r=0.45 with occlusion); ALL ankle-dependent findings must surface with explicit confidence reduction
constraints.rationale: Real-world testing shows ankle visibility compromised in forward fold and certain squat angles
constraints.implementation: Flag ankle findings as lower-confidence in report; avoid ankle-only recommendations; always pair with hip/knee context

constraints.constraint: Educational framing only — no diagnostic or treatment language. Output must frame as triage conversation starter, not clinical judgment
constraints.rationale: FDA CDS Guidance (Jan 2026); precedent from Hinge Health, Sword Health (positioned as wellness, not FDA-cleared)
constraints.implementation: Never use words: diagnosis, treatment, clinical assessment, proven. Always use: find, notice, suggests, explore with practitioner, discussion point

constraints.constraint: 3-week capstone timeline; team of 4. ML pipeline (MediaPipe, angles, chains, device optimization) is separate developer's product. App defines interface boundaries only.
constraints.rationale: Parallel development with clear ownership; app is not responsible for angle math or chain logic implementation
constraints.implementation: App provides clean abstraction interfaces: PoseEstimationService (input: video → output: landmarks + visibility scores); AngleCalculator (input: landmarks → output: joint angles); ChainMapper (input: angles + thresholds → output: flags + confidence)

constraints.constraint: Four movements only: overhead squat, single-leg balance, overhead reach, forward fold. No additions without approval.
constraints.rationale: Each movement loads system differently and forces compensations to surface; four is clinically validated minimum; additions risk scope creep and validation burden
constraints.implementation: Fixed movement order; 60 sec per movement; rep counter guidance

constraints.constraint: SBL, BFL, FFL only. Spiral, Lateral, SFL excluded.
constraints.rationale: Only three chains with strong anatomical evidence per Wilke 2016 and Kalichman 2025. SFL has zero dissection evidence. Restricting to proven chains is the only defensible position.
constraints.implementation: Chain mapper logic: IF (ankle restriction + knee valgus + hip drop) → SBL candidate. IF (shoulder depression + thoracic limitation + contralateral hip weakness) → BFL candidate. IF (ankle plantarflexion + knee extension + hip flexion dominance) → FFL candidate.

constraints.constraint: All processing on-device. Zero data transmission without explicit opt-in consent. No user accounts required for free screening.
constraints.rationale: Product spec requirement; privacy/trust positioning; aligns with FDA wellness guidance
constraints.implementation: Local storage of session data; optional Firestore save for sharing/PDF export with explicit user action

constraints.constraint: Fixed thresholds (e.g., >10° valgus) systematically misclassify hypermobile individuals (who show 3.5° lower baseline valgus). Threshold adjustment mechanism required.
constraints.rationale: PMC8558993, PMC9397026; underrepresented population in pose estimation validation
constraints.implementation: ML developer to flag: IF knee external rotation >45° during overhead squat AND valgus <5° → apply hypermobility modifier that reverses interpretation (stability emphasis vs mobility)

constraints.constraint: Every finding must cite its evidence source inline. No separate 'science' page. Citations are expandable, not cluttering.
constraints.rationale: Builds credibility through transparency; differentiates from competitor models (PostureScreen, Moti); supports regulatory position by making educational framing explicit
constraints.implementation: Report finding: 'Your knee collapses inward (valgus >10°).' [Expandable] 'This pattern correlates with ACL injury risk per Hewett 2005. Hip strengthening resolves this pattern 1 week faster than knee-only treatment (Ferber, n=199).'
